question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: liquidate (seizedAssets computation, bitmap update)] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate with repaidUnits > 0 (seizedAssets == 0 input), causing seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, liquidatedCollatPrice) to be computed, and ask whether this computed seizedAssets can exceed position.collateral[collateralIndex] due to rounding or LIF > 1, causing the uint128 subtraction to revert, and whether the bitmap bit is correctly cleared if seizedAssets == position.collateral[collateralIndex]? Invariant tested: computed seizedAssets must not exceed position.collateral[collateralIndex]; bitmap bit cleared iff collateral[i] reaches 0. Scoped impact: if seizedAssets > collateral[i], the subtraction reverts, causing DoS on liquidation; if seizedAssets == collateral[i], bit must be cleared. Proof idea: fuzz repaidUnits near collateral[i] * liquidatedCollatPrice / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / lif and assert no revert and correct bitmap update.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateral (collateralIndex == market.collateralParams.length - 1)] Can an unprivileged borrower call supplyCollateral with collateralIndex == market.collateralParams.length - 1 (the last valid index) on a market with N collateral params, causing setBit(bitmap, N-1) to set bit N-1, and ask whether the isHealthy loop correctly accesses market.collateralParams[N-1] when msb returns N-1, without reverting due to array out-of-bounds? Invariant tested: isHealthy must correctly handle all valid collateral indices 0 to market.collateralParams.length - 1. Scoped impact: if msb returns a value >= market.collateralParams.length, the array access reverts, causing DoS on health checks.
